serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of fried prawn wonton in Singapore is approximately 4 pieces, weighing around 80 g, often served as a snack or side dish at hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Fried Wanton (Prawn) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fried Wanton (Prawn), CR ID F03060015. All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on wheat-flour dumpling wrappers (GI ~52–55)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for fried prawn wonton. GI estimated at 52 based on the closest analogous food: wheat-flour-wrapped fried dumplings with mixed filling, as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 12.75) / 100 ≈ 6.6, using 80 g serving size (15.94 g carbs per 100 g → 12.75 g per 80 g serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content of 652 mg per 100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold; a single 80 g serving delivers approximately 522 mg sodium, representing over 22% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly limit fried prawn wonton to no more than 2 pieces per occasion, avoid additional soy-based dipping sauces, and request reduced seasoning when ordering freshly prepared versions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat wrapper contributes 15.94 g carbohydrates per 100 g with only 1.02 g dietary fibre, yielding a low fibre-to-carb ratio; deep-frying elevates the glycaemic response and adds 14.12 g fat per 100 g.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 2–3 pieces, avoid sweet chilli or plum dipping sauces, and prefer steamed or boiled prawn wonton as a lower-GI, lower-fat alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying contributes approximately 14.12 g total fat per 100 g including saturated fat; combined with 652 mg sodium per 100 g, this food presents dual cardiovascular risk factors of elevated dietary fat and high sodium intake.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of deep-fried prawn wonton due to its high sodium and fat content, and substitute with steamed prawn wonton which retains the protein benefit of prawns without the added frying oil or sodium from seasoning-heavy preparations.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote systemic inflammation; sodium at 652 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg RA threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals managing rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried preparations entirely and opt for steamed prawn wonton, which preserves the anti-inflammatory omega-3 content of prawns without AGE formation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried prawn wonton (炸虾云吞) is a popular Chinese-Singaporean snack and dim sum item consisting of minced prawn meat seasoned with soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and corn starch, wrapped in thin wheat-flour wonton skins and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The exterior is light and crunchy while the interior remains juicy and savoury from the prawn filling. It is widely available at Singapore hawker centres, coffee shops, and Chinese restaurants, typically served with sweet chilli sauce or plum sauce as a dipping condiment. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 239 kcal, 12.13 g protein, 14.12 g fat, and 15.94 g carbohydrates, with a notably high sodium content of 652 mg derived from seasoning sauces. The deep-frying method increases caloric density and saturated fat load. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals with hypertension due to its high sodium, for those with Type 2 Diabetes due to the refined carbohydrate wrapper and elevated glycaemic response from frying, and for cardiovascular patients due to combined fat and sodium burden. Steamed or boiled prawn wonton is a significantly healthier preparation alternative.
common_user_queries: How many calories in fried prawn wonton?
Is fried wonton high in sodium?
What is the difference between fried and steamed wonton?
How much protein in fried prawn wonton?
Is fried wonton suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F03060015) at confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest wheat-flour fried dumpling analogue, documented transparently. All mandatory multilingual names are present for a Singaporean Chinese dish.
notes: iron_mg is set to -1: the input HPB source recorded iron as -1.0 (data not available). Iron content for this specific fried prawn wonton preparation is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F03060015 as of the accessed date. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this traditional hawker preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g is estimated at 3.5 g per 100 g based on typical deep-fried prawn dumpling composition from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries, as the HPB source does not report saturated fat separately. Halal status is marked false as this product may contain oyster sauce and the halal certification status of hawker-prepared versions is not confirmed; consumers should verify with the specific vendor.
